title: rcicrely user guide
text: scaling underlying mask. Pearson r per column exactly 1 (linear scaling correlation- preserving), row-mean variance ICC formula depends ~25x larger rendered version. ICC(3,*) computed rendered data therefore ICC computed raw mask. logic applies Euclidean distance infoVal.
code: set.seed(1) n_pix <- 32L * 32L n_p   <- 20L mask  <- matrix(rnorm(n_pix * n_p, sd = 0.02), n_pix, n_p)  # Render: stretch each column to [-0.5, 0.5] (rcicr's "matched" / # auto-scale option; the rcicr default for visualisation) rendered <- apply(mask, 2L, function(m) {   rng <- diff(range(m)); if (rng == 0) m else m * (1 / rng) })  c(   raw_max_range      = round(diff(range(mask)),     3L),   rendered_max_range = round(diff(range(rendered)), 3L) ) #>      raw_max_range rendered_max_range  #>              0.162              1.159  # Pearson r between same-column raw and rendered: ~ 1 # (a perfect linear stretch preserves Pearson) mean(vapply(seq_len(n_p),             function(j) cor(mask[, j], rendered[, j]),             numeric(1L))) #> [1] 1  # But the variance ratios that drive ICC are very different var(rowMeans(mask))      # variance of the group mean over pixels #> [1] 1.923774e-05 var(rowMeans(rendered))  # same metric, on the rendered version #> [1] 0.001134698

title: rcicrely user guide
text: One row per trial. format can data.frame, data.table, tibble, anything behaves like data frame; source (CSV, RData, Parquet, programmatic construction) matter, columns present. Response coding. 2IFC: response = +1 participant chose oriented variant trial, -1 inverted variant. trial unique stimulus pair, stimulus indexes pair ranges 1:n_trials. Brief-RC 12 (Schmitz, Rougier & Yzerbyt, 2024): trial participant sees 12 noisy faces (6 oriented + 6 inverted 6 distinct underlying noise patterns) picks one. Recorded one row per trial, stimulus = pool id chosen pattern response = +1 oriented variant chosen, -1 inverted. stimulus id can repeat across trials. Watch {0, 1} miscoding. single common silent failure RC pipelines: experiment software sometimes records “left” / “right” “first” / “second” 0 / 1, analyst forgets recode. Pass {0, 1} responses ci_from_responses_*() resulting CI approximately blank (math leans heavily sign). functions error input boundary responses {-1, +1}. see error, fix one line: responses$response <- 2 * responses$response - 1. Brief-RC: record 11 unselected faces. Brief-RC trial shows 12 faces participant chooses one. Record chosen face (one row per trial, ). record 11 unselected faces additional rows response = 0, use “expanded” 12-rows-per-trial format weights chosen +1 unchosen -1/11. Neither convention matches Schmitz genMask() formula (mask = noise[, chosen] %*% chosen_responses / n_unique_chosen) ci_from_responses_briefrc() implements. Unselected faces’ noise patterns simply enter multiplication; absence zero.

code: set.seed(1) make_condition <- function(region_top, n_pix = 32L * 32L,                            n_p = 30L, snr = 0.3) {   noise <- matrix(rnorm(n_pix * n_p, sd = 0.05), n_pix, n_p)   mask <- if (region_top) {     c(rep(1, n_pix / 2L), rep(0, n_pix / 2L))   } else {     c(rep(0, n_pix / 2L), rep(1, n_pix / 2L))   }   out <- noise + snr * outer(mask, runif(n_p, 0.7, 1.3))   attr(out, "img_dims") <- c(32L, 32L)   out } sig_A <- make_condition(region_top = TRUE,  n_p = 30L) sig_B <- make_condition(region_top = FALSE, n_p = 30L)

(25 more) #>  #>   flagged producers:  none #>  #>   Note: r_loo values are near 1 by construction because the #>   full-sample and leave-one-out means share (N-1)/N of their #>   data. This is a diagnostic, not a #>   reliability statistic. r_loo_mad <- rel_loo(sig_A, flag_threshold = 2.5, flag_method = "mad") c(sd_threshold = r_loo$threshold, mad_threshold = r_loo_mad$threshold) #>  sd_threshold mad_threshold  #>     0.9999303     0.9999308 plot(r_loo) head(rel_loo_z(r_loo)) #   participant_id  correlation  z_score  flag # 1     outlier01        0.9714    -3.12  TRUE # 2           p07        0.9942    -0.84  FALSE # ...

title: rcicrely user guide
text: two examples §5 (sig_A, sig_B) use half-image signal, produces strong contrasts (useful demonstrating within-condition reliability metrics work, -condition cluster figures end looking like two solid blobs rather realistic localized clusters). §6.1–§6.2 therefore use small additional fixture localized circular signal: two small “patches” different positions 32 x 32 grid, mimicking real RC signal tends cluster around face features rather fill whole image.
code: set.seed(7) make_localized <- function(centre, n_p = 30L, n_side = 32L,                            snr = 0.20, radius = 0.18, seed = NULL) {   if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)   rr <- row(matrix(0, n_side, n_side)) / n_side - centre[1]   cc <- col(matrix(0, n_side, n_side)) / n_side - centre[2]   d  <- sqrt(rr^2 + cc^2)   m  <- as.vector(pmax(0, 1 - d / radius))   out <- snr * outer(m, runif(n_p, 0.7, 1.3)) +          matrix(rnorm(n_side^2 * n_p, sd = 0.05), n_side^2, n_p)   attr(out, "img_dims") <- c(n_side, n_side)   out } # Condition A: signal in upper-left; Condition B: signal in lower-right sig_A_loc <- make_localized(c(0.30, 0.30), n_p = 30L, seed = 11L) sig_B_loc <- make_localized(c(0.70, 0.70), n_p = 30L, seed = 12L)

what: 6.2 rel_cluster_test(): spatially-localized inference with FWER control
title: rcicrely user guide
text: . Identifies spatially contiguous regions image two conditions differ, family-wise error control across whole image (-claim significance kind mass-testing problem §6.1 warned ). vocabulary. Three terms unavoidable; one means plain language. Cluster-forming threshold (cluster_threshold, default 2.0). t-value cutoff used first step: pixel |t| > 2.0 “”, everything else “”. significance threshold; cutoff deciding pixels even candidates part cluster. Significance decided afterwards. Lower thresholds catch diffuse signal; higher thresholds isolate sharper peaks. 4-connectivity. binary “/ ” image, group adjacent “” pixels clusters. 4-connectivity treats two pixels adjacent share edge (/ / left / right), .e. four orthogonal neighbours. 8-connectivity, alternative, also counts diagonals. 4-connectivity conservative choice: one-pixel-wide diagonal stripe split isolated pixels forms cluster, whereas 8-connectivity chain . Suits face-CI signal, tends contiguous orthogonal directions. Cluster mass (mass). candidate cluster, mass sum t-values within . pixel count. means small cluster strong t-values can mass large cluster weak t-values; metric mixes extent (big) magnitude (strong). Cluster mass introduced Maris & Oostenveld (2007) dominant choice EEG / MEG / fMRI cluster-permutation work. Stratified label permutation. build null distribution, randomly relabel producers belong vs. B many times, recompute t-map, find clusters, record maximum cluster mass per permutation. “Stratified” means preserve original group sizes (N_A, N_B) exactly time, Welch denominator perturbed H0. paired = TRUE, permutation sign-flip matched pairs instead. title: rcicrely user guide
text: compute infoval() find per-producer z-scores sit well 1.96 (sometimes negative) even though spot checks suggest producers task seriously, common pattern, sign data bad. Five reasons order often apply: Frobenius norm global energy statistic. sums squared pixel deviations across entire image. Real internal representations usually spatially sparse (eyes, mouth, jaw, maybe 10-30% pixels), 70-90% “background” pixels contribute noise similar magnitude chance reference dilute signal-bearing region. producer strong, visually-obvious signal eyes can Frobenius norm marginally random reference. reference strict lives subspace. observed mask reference projections onto low-dimensional sinusoidal noise basis. reference distribution plenty overall energy construction, way clear z = 1.96 align signs specific subset patterns chance. Per-trial signal small. 2IFC choice contributes tiny signal increment relative per-trial noise amplitude. 300 trials SNR gain √300 ≈ 17×, per-trial signal ~5% per-trial noise, post-aggregation effective SNR barely visible global energy measure. Without face mask, infoVal counts background. face_mask() ships oval mask approximates Schmitz 2024 face region. Applying (infoval(..., mask =    face_mask(c(256, 256)))) concentrates norm signal-bearing pixels typically lifts z-scores noticeably. Group-level CIs much higher z individual CIs. Averaging 20 producers’ masks reduces noise √20 ≈ 4.5×, group-mean CI’s effective trial count 300 × 20 = 6000 20-producer condition. infoVal group-mean CI (trial_counts = setNames(300L * n_producers, "group")) usually 5-10× per-producer median; structural √N consequence averaging, defect per-producer metric. Brinkman et al. (2019) ever computed infoVal individual CIs (mean per-producer infoVal empirical 2IFC data 3.9 lab / 2.9 online, 68% / 54% producers individually exceeding 1.96). group-level reporting recommended inspecting distribution per-producer infoVals contributing group CI rather computing one infoVal group-mean CI. Either choice defensible; show group-mean CI z simplest single number, distributional view 2019 paper endorses.

code: sm     <- res$signal_matrix tc     <- setNames(rep(300L, ncol(sm)), colnames(sm))  # 1. Compare the observed and reference norm distributions iv  <- infoval(sm, noise_matrix, tc, iter = 1000L,                cache_path = "iv.rds", seed = 1L) ref <- iv$reference[[as.character(tc[1])]] cat(sprintf("observed median = %.4f, reference median = %.4f, %% above = %+.1f%%\n",             median(iv$norms), median(ref),             100 * (median(iv$norms) - median(ref)) / median(ref)))  # 2. Apply the face mask fm  <- face_mask(c(256L, 256L)) iv_masked <- infoval(sm, noise_matrix, tc, mask = fm,                      iter = 1000L, cache_path = "iv_masked.rds",                      seed = 1L) median(iv_masked$infoval)        # typically higher than iv  # 3. Group-mean CI (the headline number to report) group   <- matrix(rowMeans(sm), ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, "group")) tc_grp  <- setNames(300L * ncol(sm), "group") iv_grp  <- infoval(group, noise_matrix, tc_grp, iter = 1000L,                    seed = 1L) iv_grp$infoval                   # usually large (e.g. > 5)  # 4. Sanity check with a simulated random responder set.seed(0) random_resp <- sample(c(-1, 1), 300L, replace = TRUE) random_mask <- (noise_matrix %*% random_resp) / 300 iv_rand <- infoval(matrix(random_mask, ncol = 1,                           dimnames = list(NULL, "rand")),                    noise_matrix,                    setNames(300L, "rand"),                    iter = 1000L, seed = 1L) iv_rand$infoval                  # should be ~ 0 within MAD noise

title: rcicrely user guide
text: Load response data noise pool. pattern works 2IFC study subject, trial, stimulus, response columns (column names vary datasets; rename fit). Build signal matrix one trait (Trust). producer’s raw mask sign-weighted average noise patterns chose: mask = (noise[, chosen_stimuli] %*% chosen_responses) / n_trials Repeat block (lines 4 onwards) "DOMINANT", "COMPETENT", "FRIENDLY" get sm_dominant, sm_competent, sm_friendly. 10 traits one pass, wrap loop function:
code: library(rcicrely) library(dplyr)  # Trial-level data: one row per (subject, trial) pair. responses <- read.csv2("study1data.csv") glimpse(responses) #> $ subject  <int> ...           # producer id #> $ trait    <chr> "TRUST" ...    # condition label #> $ trial    <int> 1, 2, 3, ... #> $ stimulus <int> ...            # noise pattern shown on this trial #> $ response <int> +1 / -1        # +1 = oriented chosen, -1 = inverted  # Pixels x pool-size matrix of noise patterns used at stimulus generation. noise_matrix <- read_noise_matrix("rcic_stimuli.Rdata", baseimage = "male") dim(noise_matrix) #> 65536 x 300 # Filter to Trust trials, ordered by producer + trial number. trust_trials <- responses %>%   filter(trait == "TRUST") %>%   arrange(subject, trial)  producers <- unique(trust_trials$subject)             # 20 ids n_pixels  <- nrow(noise_matrix) sm_trust  <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n_pixels, ncol = length(producers)) colnames(sm_trust) <- as.character(producers)  # One column per producer: their raw mask. for (i in seq_along(producers)) {   this_producer <- trust_trials %>% filter(subject == producers[i])   sm_trust[, i] <- (noise_matrix[, this_producer$stimulus] %*%                       this_producer$response) / nrow(this_producer) } attr(sm_trust, "img_dims") <- c(256L, 256L) attr(sm_trust, "source")   <- "raw" build_sm <- function(trait_label) {   sub <- responses %>%     filter(trait == trait_label) %>%     arrange(subject, trial)   ids <- unique(sub$subject)   m   <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n_pixels, ncol = length(ids))   colnames(m) <- as.character(ids)   for (i in seq_along(ids)) {     pr      <- sub %>% filter(subject == ids[i])     m[, i]  <- (noise_matrix[, pr$stimulus] %*% pr$response) / nrow(pr)   }   attr(m, "img_dims") <- c(256L, 256L)   attr(m, "source")   <- "raw"   m }  signal_by_trait <- lapply(unique(responses$trait), build_sm) names(signal_by_trait) <- unique(responses$trait)  sm_trust     <- signal_by_trait[["TRUST"]] sm_dominant  <- signal_by_trait[["DOMINANT"]] sm_competent <- signal_by_trait[["COMPETENT"]] sm_friendly  <- signal_by_trait[["FRIENDLY"]]

title: rcicrely user guide
text: Region-restricted ICC(3,1) Trust signal matrix shows producer agreement concentrates: Agreement strongest lower-face region (mouth + jaw), weakest nose. pattern aligns visible features published Trust group CI (Figure 1 paper). Region- restricted infoVal works sensitivity check data: Eye mouth regions replicate full-face median; eye region’s per-producer range narrower, suggesting producers agree direction eye region energy modest. Region-restricted -condition clusters (Trustworthy vs Dominant), zero-filling outside region clusters can form within : Region-restricted cluster test, Trustworthy vs Dominant group CIs (Oliveira et al., 2019, Study 1). Pixel-wise Welch t-map rendered translucent diverging overlay male base face; positive/red = Trustworthy larger, negative/blue = Dominant larger. Eyes region (left panel) mouth region (right panel). Significant clusters FWE-corrected alpha = .05 outlined black.
code: # rel_icc(sm_trust[face_mask(c(256L,256L), region = R), , drop = FALSE]) #> region        n_pixels   ICC(3,1)   ICC(3,k) #> full           32,192     0.109      0.710 #> eyes            1,022     0.069      0.595 #> nose              770     0.028      0.363 #> mouth             966     0.092      0.670 #> upper_face     16,096     0.087      0.655 #> lower_face     16,096     0.130      0.749 # infoval(sm_trust, noise_matrix, tc, mask = face_mask(c(256L,256L), region = R), ...) #> region   median z   range          n above 1.96 #> full     +0.51      [-1.81, +3.13]      4/20 #> eyes     +0.55      [-0.98, +1.56]      0/20 #> mouth    +0.52      [-0.76, +4.67]      3/20 img_dims <- c(256L, 256L) m_eyes   <- face_mask(img_dims, region = "eyes") m_mouth  <- face_mask(img_dims, region = "mouth")  # rel_cluster_test() needs the full image grid to label connected # components, so we restrict by "blanking out" pixels outside the # region: set them to zero. Clusters can then only form inside the # kept region. zero_fill <- function(sm, keep_pixels) {   out <- sm   out[!keep_pixels, ] <- 0   out }  # Eyes region. sm_trust_eyes    <- zero_fill(sm_trust,    m_eyes) sm_dominant_eyes <- zero_fill(sm_dominant, m_eyes) rel_cluster_test(sm_trust_eyes, sm_dominant_eyes,                  img_dims          = img_dims,                  cluster_threshold = 2.0,                  n_permutations    = 1000L,                  seed              = 1L)  # Mouth region. sm_trust_mouth    <- zero_fill(sm_trust,    m_mouth) sm_dominant_mouth <- zero_fill(sm_dominant, m_mouth) rel_cluster_test(sm_trust_mouth, sm_dominant_mouth,                  img_dims          = img_dims,                  cluster_threshold = 2.0,                  n_permutations    = 1000L,                  seed              = 2L)

title: rcicrely user guide
text: Schmitz, Rougier & Yzerbyt (2024) introduced Brief-RC 12: trial shows 12 noisy faces (6 oriented, 6 inverted, drawn shared noise pool); participant picks one. recorded format one row per trial, columns participant_id, trial, stimulus (chosen pool id), response (+1 oriented, -1 inverted). rcicrely’s implementation native (rcicr _brief functions, don’t exist upstream rcicr v1.0.1). Schmitz materials distribute noise pool plain-text noise_matrix.txt (pixels × pool size, whitespace-delimited), ’s typical entry point replicate OSF repo. generate pool rcicr, captured return value can cached .rds fast reloads (see step 1 ). Brief-RC infoVal ships v0.2 infoval() (see §6.5). reference distribution matched producer’s recorded trial count (number (stim, +/-1) contributions entering mask), pool size. 2IFC two equal original rcicr default fine; Brief-RC producer typically records fewer choices pool items, pool-size reference biases infoVal downward. infoval() closes calibration gap. Pass raw mask (res$signal_matrix), res$rendered_ci PNG. Two notes interpretation. First, “subset” means number recorded choices enter mask (one (stim, +/-1) per trial), producer’s perceptual exposure: Brief-RC trials cognitively richer 2IFC trials (12 noisy faces context vs. 2), producers see less pool. trial-count match right calibration infoVal’s magnitude statistic, claim cognitive exposure. Second, infoVal measures magnitude mask (Frobenius norm) chance reference, distinguish accurate mask larger one. Treat absolute Brief-RC vs 2IFC z-score differences caution; rel_split_half()/rel_icc() (signal stability) rel_cluster_test()/rel_dissimilarity() (-condition separability) right complements pattern accuracy matters magnitude.
code: # 1a. Replicating Schmitz et al.: read the published noise_matrix.txt #     directly. read_noise_matrix() autodetects the format. noise <- read_noise_matrix("data/noise_matrix.txt")  # 1b. Generating your own pool with rcicr (one-off, slow): rcicr::generateStimuli2IFC(   base_face_files     = list(base = "data/base.jpg"),   n_trials            = 600L,        # becomes the Brief-RC pool size   img_size            = 256L,   stimulus_path       = "data/stim",   label               = "mystudy",   ncores              = 1L,          # macOS-safe default   return_as_dataframe = TRUE,        # capture the noise matrix   seed                = 1L ) |> saveRDS("data/noise_matrix.rds")  # cache for fast reloads  # 2. Collect Brief-RC responses in the per-trial format.  # 3. Compute individual CIs. res <- ci_from_responses_briefrc(   responses       = my_brief_rc_responses,   noise_matrix    = noise,           # or readRDS("data/noise_matrix.rds")   base_image_path = "data/base.jpg",   method          = "briefrc12",   scaling         = "matched"        # also rendered for plotting )  # 4. Reliability assessment. within  <- run_within(res$signal_matrix, seed = 1L) between <- run_between(res_A$signal_matrix, res_B$signal_matrix,                        seed = 1L)  # 5. Save rendered CIs to PNG (visualisation only; do not feed to rel_*). if (requireNamespace("png", quietly = TRUE)) {   for (j in seq_along(res$participants)) {     img <- matrix(res$rendered_ci[, j], res$img_dims[1], res$img_dims[2])     img <- pmin(pmax(img, 0), 1)     png::writePNG(img, sprintf("plots/p%02d.png", j))   } }

title: Per-producer informational value with trial-count-matched reference — infoval
text: observed statistic Frobenius norm producer j's classification image mask, optionally restricted logical mask (e.g. oval face region via face_mask()):   reference distribution producer j built simulating iter random masks trial count trial_counts[j], mirroring genMask() construction exactly (Schmitz, Rougier, & Yzerbyt, 2024): Sample trial_counts[j] stimulus ids uniformly 1:n_pool (without replacement trial_counts[j] <= n_pool, typical case 2IFC standard Brief-RC; replacement producer recorded contributions pool size). See v0.2.1 calibration note . Sample trial_counts[j] responses uniformly {-1, +1} (always replacement). Collapse response stim via mean() (duplicate-stim rule used Brief-RC). Build mask: noise_matrix[, unique_stims] %*% mean_response / n_unique_stims. Apply mask (supplied) compute Frobenius norm. per-producer z-score (norm_j - median(ref)) / mad(ref), reference distribution one matched producer j's trial count. Producers sharing trial count share reference; makes simulation efficient several producers number trials. Choice statistic: Frobenius norm correct form per Schmitz, Rougier, & Yzerbyt (2020, comment) 2020 erratum. original rcicr::computeInfoVal2IFC() already uses (norm(matrix(target_ci[["ci"]]), "f")); function matches convention 2IFC Brief-RC data. Trial-count matching: original rcicr::generateReferenceDistribution2IFC() builds reference using full pool size (n_trials = ncol(noise_matrix)). 2IFC appropriate every producer typically responds every pool item, per-producer trial count equals pool size. Brief-RC producer's cognitive exposure per trial richer 2IFC (12 noisy faces vs 2), one chosen (stim, +/-1) recorded per trial, number mask contributions n_trials typically smaller n_pool. pool-size reference therefore mismatch Brief-RC biases infoVal downward. infoval() closes gap keying reference actual per-producer recorded trial count. infoVal measures (). Frobenius norm magnitude statistic, target-alignment one: answers "mask larger chance?" "pointing right pattern?". Two consequences worth flagging interpreting per-producer z-scores: Cross-paradigm comparisons need care. Brief-RC 2IFC placed z-scale infoval() (per-producer trial- count-matched reference, identical mask construction), cognitive processes generating masks differ. producer benefits Brief-RC's richer per-trial context might produce accurately localized necessarily larger mask, magnitude metric reward . Treat absolute Brief-RC vs 2IFC z-score differences caution. Stability discriminability different questions. rel_split_half() / rel_icc() ask whether signal stable; rel_cluster_test() / rel_dissimilarity() ask whether conditions separable. right complements magnitude-based infoVal.

title: Cluster-based permutation test with family-wise error control — rel_cluster_test
text: Common scaffold (methods): Compute observed pixel-wise Welch t (see pixel_t_test()). Build null via n_permutations stratified label permutations, preserving (N_A, N_B) exactly. Max-statistic null controls FWER strong sense. Method-specific step: method = "threshold" (default): threshold |t| > cluster_threshold, label connected components 4-connectivity, cluster mass = sum t-values within cluster. Per observed cluster, p-value = fraction null max-masses (matching sign) equal exceed observed mass absolute value. method = "tfce": enhance observed t-map TFCE map (integral thresholds size^E * h^H dh, pos neg tails enhanced separately recombined sign preserved; H = 2.0, E = 0.5 default). Compute enhancement permuted t-map record max(|TFCE|). Per-pixel p-value = fraction null max-TFCE values equal exceed observed |TFCE| pixel. free threshold parameter.

what: Why Euclidean and not Pearson correlation as the primary
title: Between-condition dissimilarity with bootstrap confidence intervals — rel_dissimilarity
text: earlier release emphasised Pearson correlation two group-mean CIs co-primary metric. current release retains fields backwards compatibility ; scheduled removal v0.3 reported primary new work. reason positive baseline: two base-subtracted CIs share systematic image-domain spatial structure (face shape, oval signal support, low-frequency Gaussian-noise smoothness) pushes correlation zero even underlying mental representations unrelated. Absolute correlation values therefore cleanly mean "conditions similar"; conflate real similarity shared image-domain structure. Euclidean distance share failure mode. honest magnitude summary far two group-mean CIs sit pixel-signal units. cross-study comparisons, use $euclidean_normalised (divided sqrt(n_pixels) metric resolution-invariant).

what: Raw vs. rendered CIs - documentation overhaul + safety net
title: rcicrely 0.1.1 (unreleased)
text: Reliability metrics operate raw mask (participant’s unscaled noise contribution). Reading PNGs disk via read_cis() silently loaded rendered CI (base + scaling(mask)) fed scaling(mask) downstream metrics, can distort variance-based statistics (rel_icc(), Euclidean dissimilarity, pixel_t_test(), rel_cluster_test()). standard 2IFC infoVal path (rcicr::computeInfoVal2IFC()) unaffected extracts raw $ci element rcicr CI-list internally; hand-rolled infoVal implementations (Brief-RC, custom code) affected need raw mask. mathematical core package always correct; release sharpens boundary users see issue. read_cis(), extract_signal(), load_signal_matrix() now emit -per-session cli warning explaining raw-vs-rendered distinction, metrics affected, silence (acknowledge_scaling = TRUE argument options(rcicrely.silence_scaling_warning = TRUE)). Every rel_*() run_*() function now runs cheap heuristic entry (looks_scaled()) emits -per-session warning input matrix’s dynamic range looks rendered rather raw. Heuristic ; silencing options. vignette("tutorial") restructured function--function user guide (~600 lines, ~350). Adds dedicated “Mental model: signal matrix” chapter numerical demonstration scaling distorts variance-based metrics leaving Pearson correlation intact, “Interpreting results” chapter flags infoVal pitfall. README.md adds top-level “Important: raw vs. rendered CIs” callout.

Result object gains $median_r, $mad_r, $flag_method, $flag_threshold fields.

title: rcicrely 0.1.0
text: rel_split_half() - permuted split-half Spearman-Brown correction; handles odd N via per-permutation drop. rel_loo() - leave-one-sensitivity 2.5-SD outlier flagging default. rel_icc() - ICC(3,1) ICC(3,k) via direct mean-squares (two-way mixed model: pixels fixed, participants random); ICC(2,*) available request. pixel_t_test() - vectorised Welch’s t per pixel. rel_cluster_test() - cluster-based permutation test 4-connectivity labelling max-statistic FWER control; permutation stratified (preserves N_A, N_B). rel_dissimilarity() - bootstrap correlation + Euclidean distance percentile CIs (boot dependency). run_within(), run_between() - run every metric, wrap rcicrely_report. read_cis(), extract_signal(), load_signal_matrix() pre-computed CI images. read_noise_matrix() rcicr .Rdata plain-text noise matrices. ci_from_responses_2ifc() - wraps rcicr::batchGenerateCI2IFC() known gotchas handled. ci_from_responses_briefrc() - native Brief-RC 12 implementation per Schmitz, Rougier & Yzerbyt (2024), matching genMask() exactly including duplicate-stimulus tie-breaking. S3 methods - print, summary, plot every rcicrely_* result class plus rcicrely_report.
